title: Nour and the Stony Ground
logline: Nour plants a hidden seed in Sidqi, the hardest ground of all.
story: Nothing had ever grown in Sidqi, who was truthfulness, un-concealment — ground so hard the others laughed when Nour, who carried light, stillness, was handed a single seed and told to plant it there. Nour did not argue; Nour simply returned each day to tend the barren place. No green came for a long season, and still Nour watered the stone. Then one morning a thread of root had split the rock from within. What force could not break, patience had quietly opened.
moral: What force cannot break, steady and patient tending opens from within.
attribution: a Pagarek parable, from Project Genesis
